rows.subject: one click, whole team
rows.last_client_message_text:
Hey, 
There's a tiny feature in Keeping that customers absolutely love, and it's surprisingly simple. 
It's the ability to push an email from your personal inbox into the shared inbox with a single click. 
Here's why it matters: as your team grows, customers will inevitably email individual team members directly instead of using the support address. 
That email becomes one person's problem, sitting on their to-do list. 
But with Keeping's "Convert to Ticket" feature, that email can be immediately shared with the entire team. One click, and it's no longer a single point of failure. 
This is especially helpful as you scale. It removes the overwhelm that happens when one person becomes the bottleneck because customers keep emailing them directly. 
What was once buried in someone's personal inbox is now visible to the whole team, and anyone can jump in to help. 
One click. Whole team. Problem solved. 
Get started with Keeping 
Keep on keeping on, 
Cody 
Founder, Keeping 
Feel free to unsubscribe if you would no longer like to receive these updates.

Dear Michael & Adil
Please see below report from the chap who replaced the battery. Thank you so much for looking after it :-)

Begin forwarded message:
From: Richard Ellis McCallum < rich@aandemacwrks.com >
Date: 12 November 2025 at 15:51:12 GMT
To: Guy Mantoura < g.mantoura@gmail.com >
Subject: Re: Message via the website... 
Hi Guy,
Okay, this afternoon I decided to fit your new battery with a view to returning it tomorrow afternoon.
So this is what your MacBook Pro looked like when I removed the base cover…
So here you can see both fans are pretty clogged up with dust and fluff... 
Did you notice any increased fan noise or excessive heat when you were using it?
I cleaned all the fans and checked the inside visually before proceeding any further.
The batteries in these MacBooks are comprised of six parts running along the bottom of the machine. Each part is glued in place and has to be treated individually. To remove them requires the use of solvent and to do that safely, some dismantling of the machine is required and the trackpad has to be removed altogether, because it sits directly above the battery. 
I have now removed the old battery and fitted the new, but I’m afraid it hasn’t fixed the problem. Your machine replicates the same issues I have seen lately with two or three machines coming in apparently ‘just dying’ for no obvious reason. They have all been manufactured from 2018-2020. Yours was slightly different because we already knew that the battery was displaying the ’Service Recommended’ message. But this was also why I asked the questions about its history before agreeing to take it on.
I’ll try to explain what I believe is going on. As you may know, unlike most previous Macs, this model comes with a charging cable that does not have light indicators to simply tell us what is going on. To get around that I have a little tool as shown below.
This is your MacBook Pro plugged into a charger after I fitted the battery - please note the 5.19V figure...
For comparison purposes and using the exact same charger, this is what I see on another 15in MacBook Pro I have serviced and rebuilt for sale. This one is from 2017…
S
